description:
Foundation landed in f46dd968: Descr::arrow_join_return helper + cont_slot0_descr extension for Term::CallClosure. No visible codegen diff yet — the forces still dominate.

## Blocker discovered

fz_spawn_entry (src/ir_codegen.rs:1605-1631) hardcodes halt_cont_body_tagged for spawned closures. After dropping closure_target_fns from tagged_return_fns seed (so closure bodies return RawInt/RawF64 narrow), spawned closure bodies tail-call into a Tagged halt-cont that unboxes 42 → sshr_imm 3 → 5. Real test failure: runtime::tests::spawn_enqueues_child_task.

## Two resolution paths

(a) Closure-header halt-kind. MakeClosure writes body's return repr into a closure-header slot (or extends the schema id encoding). fz_spawn_entry reads it at runtime, picks the matching halt_cont_body_{tagged,i64,f64}. Generalizes cleanly to fz_resume_park too (mailbox-receive cont chain has the same closing-seam issue).

(b) Pre-spawn halt-cont selection in IR. Codegen at the fz_spawn callsite (today: a Call to runtime fz_spawn(closure)) inspects the closure's body sid via fn_constants, computes the right halt-cont id, and either:
  - calls a typed fz_spawn variant (3-way: fz_spawn_tagged, fz_spawn_i64, fz_spawn_f64), or
  - passes the halt-kind as an arg to a unified fz_spawn(closure, halt_kind).

(a) is simpler at the call site (one fz_spawn ABI) but adds runtime indirection.
(b) is simpler at runtime (no extra load) but proliferates spawn entry points.

## Next steps

Pick (a) or (b), then re-attempt the codegen force-drop. Add expect_clif_excludes assertions on closure-using fixtures locking in the narrow body return seam.

## Out of scope (still)

Arg-slot narrowing — see fz-ul4.27.22.7.
title: fz-cps-seam.return: closure body return-slot narrowing via typer cont_slot0_descr
